PreceptDef.label: bench trial
PreceptDef.description: Justice is too volatile and critical a matter to be left to the emotional whims of the common crowd. A single, clear-headed magistrate must weigh the evidence and dictate the absolute truth.

PreceptDef.label: jury trial
PreceptDef.description: No individual is infallible, and absolute power over life and liberty corrupts. A collective panel of one's peers must weigh the truth together to ensure true, uncorrupted justice.
